reasoning: The inflation rate (CPI) for February 2025 printed at 2.8% year-over-year, following a hot January print (+0.5% MoM). While February's +0.2% MoM was slightly softer than the expected +0.3%, returning the YoY rate to below 2.5% by the end of 2025 faces strong headwinds. During mid-2024, inflation printed at 0.0% and -0.1%, meaning base effects in mid-2025 will mechanically push the YoY rate higher. Furthermore, impending tariffs under the Trump administration are widely expected by economists to be inflationary, putting upward pressure on consumer prices. The Manifold predictor market valued this at roughly 23% in early March before the February print. Taking into account the modest cooling in the latest data but the broader upward trajectory dictated by base effects and trade policy, a sub-2.5% reading on CPI remains unlikely but possible in a strong disinflation scenario. I estimate the probability at 24%.
